steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the volume formula for a rectangular prism
steps.content:
The volume of a right rectangular prism is found by multiplying its three dimensions together. We're given:
  • Length = 5 cm
  • Width = 8 cm
  • Height = 12 cm

steps.title: Substitute the values and calculate
steps.content:
Substitute each measurement into the formula:
$$V = 5 \times 8 \times 12$$
Calculate by multiplying step by step:
$$5 \times 8 = 40$$
$$40 \times 12 = 480$$
The volume is 480 cubic centimeters.
